items.title: Over the last seven seasons (since The Bubble), the three teams that have exceeded their 3pt Shot Quality in the playoffs the most by eFG%:
items.author: Couper Moorhead
items.body_excerpt:
Over the last seven seasons (since The Bubble), the three teams that have exceeded their 3pt Shot Quality in the playoffs the most by eFG%:

1. Knicks (25-26) at +7.69%
2. Heat (22-23) at +5.23%
3. Pacers (24-25) at +4.93%

(Min 500 3pt FGA)

items.title: Great note from Justin Kubatko at @statitudes.com: "The Knicks have won each of their last six road playoff games by double figures, breaking a tie with the 2013 Heat and the 2017 Warriors for the longest such streak in NBA postseason history." www.statitudes.com/p/nba-game-n...

items.title: For the fourth year in a row, the Eastern Conference champion lost in the ECF the year before:
items.author: NBA Stat
items.body_excerpt:
For the fourth year in a row, the Eastern Conference champion lost in the ECF the year before:

2026: Knicks (lost in '25 ECF)
2025: Pacers (lost in '24 ECF)
2024: Celtics (lost in '23 ECF)
2023: Heat (lost in '22 ECF)

(via UnderdogNBA)
#NBA

items.title: Believe it or not, Spurs-Thunder is the first NBA conference final to be tied 2-2 after four games since 2022 (Celtics-Heat) -- and first WCF to be tied since 2018 (Rockets-Warriors).
items.author: Sports Media Watch
items.body_excerpt:
Believe it or not, Spurs-Thunder is the first NBA conference final to be tied 2-2 after four games since 2022 (Celtics-Heat) -- and first WCF to be tied since 2018 (Rockets-Warriors).

This is only the second time since *2014* that the WCF has been tied 2-2.

items.title: Hollinger POTD Rasaul Butler. LaSalle SF was drafted 53rd in 2002 by MIA. Made the team as a shooter who played some defense. Scored 7 ppg before trade to NO in 2005.  Started from 2008-10 & peaked at 12 ppg. Bounced around from 2011-16 before retiring. Sadly died in 2018. RIP.
items.author: HoopsAnalyst
items.body_excerpt: Hollinger POTD Rasaul Butler. LaSalle SF was drafted 53rd in 2002 by MIA. Made the team as a shooter who played some defense. Scored 7 ppg before trade to NO in 2005.  Started from 2008-10 & peaked at 12 ppg. Bounced around from 2011-16 before retiring. Sadly died in 2018. RIP.

items.title: All of the top-5 defenses had at least one All-Defense selection
items.author: Law Murray 📘
items.body_excerpt:
All of the top-5 defenses had at least one All-Defense selection

1. OKC (Holmgren, Wallace)
2. DET (Thompson)
3. SA (Wembanyama)
4. BOS (White)
5. TOR (Barnes)

Other 4 selections/what team's defense ranked:

7. NY (Anunoby)
8. MIN (Gobert)
10. ATL (Daniels)
14. MIA (Adebayo)

items.body_excerpt:
NBA All-Defensive Teams

First Team
Victor Wembanyama (Spurs)
Chet Holmgren (Thunder)
Ausar Thompson (Pistons)
Rudy Gobert (Timberwolves)
Derrick White (Celtics)

Second Team
Bam Adebayo (Heat)
OG Anunoby (Knicks)
Scottie Barnes (Raptors)
Cason Wallace (Thunder)
Dyson Daniels (Hawks)

items.title: The Chicago Bulls will interview Miami Heat assistant coach Chris Quinn as part of their head coaching search, league sources told @hoopshypeofficial.bsky.social. Quinn has a strong background in player development and has worked as an assistant coach on Erik Spoelstra’s staff si
items.author: Michael Scotto
items.body_excerpt: The Chicago Bulls will interview Miami Heat assistant coach Chris Quinn as part of their head coaching search, league sources told @hoopshypeofficial.bsky.social. Quinn has a strong background in player development and has worked as an assistant coach on Erik Spoelstra’s staff since 2014.
